parameters.content:
## 3. Split PDF Pages into Seperate Images

Currently, the vision model we'll be using can't accept raw PDFs so we'll have to convert our PDF to a image in order to use it. To achieve this, we'll use the free [Stirling PDF webservice]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} for convenience but if we need data privacy (recommended!), we could self-host our own [Stirling PDF instance]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} instead. Alternatively, feel free to swap this service out for one of your own as long as it can convert PDFs into images!

We will ask the PDF service to return each page of our statement as separate images, which it does so as a zip file. Next steps is to just unzip the file and convert the output as a list of images.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
## 4. Convert PDF Pages to Markdown Using Vision Model
[Learn more about using the Basic LLM node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Pitch decks are fundamentally extravagant sales documents and as such, are incredibly varied in how they are styled, structured and presented. Traditional OCR has often struggled with parsing these kinds of documents as layers and graphical elements and when extracting their contents typically yields poor results; either garbled or missing texts.

Multimodal LLMs are a solution as they use AI vision to "read" the pitch deck and can reason about it's layout and intent. Images can be understood and described with context and charts and graphs can also be interpreted. In this demonstration, we'll ask our LLM to transcribe each page in the pitch deck into markdown ensuring it also describes any images or charts it sees.

parameters.content:
## 6. Build a Vector Store Collection for the Pitch Deck
[Read more about Qdrant Vector Store]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

When it comes to pitch deck, it may not be enough to capture static attributes in table. Wouldn't it be cool if we could also ask questions about the pitch deck itself? Well, thanks for n8n's first class support for vector stores, you can and quite easily too!

In this demonstration, we'll use the Qdrant Vector Store which you can either sign-up for a free instance at {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or self-host via docker. Next, it's a simple case of just funneling our transcribed pages into the vector store using the Qdrant Vector Store node.

options.systemPromptTemplate:
You are playing the role of a Venture Capitalist with the following persona:

Location: San Francisco, California, USA

## Background 
* Education: Bachelor's in Finance from the University of California, Berkeley. MBA from Stanford Graduate School of Business.
* Career: Started as an investment banker at Goldman Sachs, then transitioned into venture capital as a junior partner at a mid-sized VC firm in Silicon Valley. Founded his own VC firm, Harper Capital, after several successful investments in early-stage startups. With over 15 years of experience, Jim has built a reputation for identifying promising startups and helping them scale.
* Track Record: His early investments in fintech companies like Plaid and Robinhood brought significant returns. Currently managing a $500 million fund focused on early-stage startups.
* Industry Focus: Fintech

Jim is particularly passionate about financial technology startups, especially those disrupting traditional banking, payment systems, and lending. He’s interested in companies that promote financial inclusion, simplify personal finance, or democratize investing. He believes the next major financial revolution will come from blockchain and decentralized finance (DeFi) platforms, but he remains cautious about overhyped cryptocurrencies.

## Investment Style:
* Stage: Seed to Series A
* Check Size: $1 million to $10 million
* Preferred Business Models: B2B SaaS and platform-driven fintech solutions
* Founder Criteria: He looks for passionate, gritty founders who deeply understand the financial system and can navigate regulatory complexities.

## Personality:

### Strengths:
* Analytical: Jim is highly data-driven and excels at performing thorough due diligence, meticulously analyzing financial projections and market data.
Hands-on Mentor: He takes an active role in the companies he invests in, offering strategic guidance on business models, scaling, and leadership development.
* Networked: Jim has deep connections with major banks, hedge funds, and technology partners, helping his startups form crucial partnerships.
Personality Weakness: Overly Risk-Averse

While Jim is known for his sharp financial acumen, he often hesitates to invest in more disruptive, experimental technologies. This risk aversion can cause him to miss out on breakthrough opportunities in early, unproven markets. He tends to favor startups with proven business models over "moonshot" ideas, which has occasionally led to regrets about passing on high-risk, high-reward ventures.
Jim Harper's experience, focus on fintech, and a disciplined investment approach have made him a trusted name in venture capital, though his cautious nature sometimes keeps him on the sidelines during tech's biggest waves.
--

Analyse the pitch deck and provide an executive summary, fact checking review and judgement of if the pitching startup would be of interest to you based on your experience and investment strategy.

For any property not found, leave blank.

parameters.inputSchema:
{
	"type": "object",
	"properties": {
    	"startup_name": { "type": "string" },
        "founders": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string" } },
		"founding_year": { "type": "string" },
        "team_size": { "type": "number" },
        "location": { "type": "string" },
        "address": { "type": "string" },
        "phone": { "type": "string" },
        "email": { "type": "string" },
        "linkedin": { "type": "string" },
        "twitter": { "type": "string" },

        "funding_stage": { "type": "string" },
        "current_investors": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string" } },
        "amount_raised": { "type": "string" },
        "current_revenue": { "type": "string" },
        "current_number_of_signups": { "type": "number" },
        "current_number_of_DAU": { "type": "number" },

        "business_model": { "type": "string" },
        "geographical_markets": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string" } },
        "verticals": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string" } },   
        "value_proposition": { "type": "string" },
        "market_validation_summary": { "type": "string" },
        "traction": { "type": "string", "description": "a summary of the amount of traction claimed" },
        "amount_requested": { "type": "string", "description": "A range" },
        "compatible_with_venture_capital": { "type": "boolean" },

        "executive_summary": { "type": "string" },
        "items_requiring_factchecking":  { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string" } },
        "is_interesting": { "type": "boolean" }
 	}
}

nodes.name: Transcribe to Markdown

messageValues.message:
=You help transcribe documents to markdown, keeping faithful to all text printed and visible to the best of your ability.
* Ensure you capture all headings, subheadings, titles as well as small print.
* For any tables found with the document, convert them to markdown tables. If table row descriptions overflow into more than 1 row, concatanate and fit them into a single row. If two or more tables are adjacent horizontally, stack the tables vertically instead. There should be a newline after every markdown table.
* For any charts, describe the chart type, purpose and result and capture all relevant titles, labels, legends and generaet a table for the datapoints if possible.
* For images, describe the image along with any captions.
* Label headers and footers with "HEADER:" and "FOOTER:" respectively.

nodes.notes: This chainLlm node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Ask Questions About Pitchdecks

options.systemMessage:
You help the user answer questions about a startup's pitch deck if it is available in our knowledge base. Assume all user questions are referring to the pitchdecks. Only use the knowledge base to answer questions. If you cannot find the requested information in the knowledge base, then let the user know.

Before answering any questions, ensure the user has specified a startup in which they want to enquire about and that the startup pitchdeck exists in the database. If the pitchdeck is not known to us, let the user know.

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note8

parameters.content:
## Try It Out!

### This n8n template imports Pitch Decks and generates a report into Airtable as well as creates an AI Chatbot to ask questions about each Pitch Deck.

* Airtable is used as the pitch deck database and PDF decks are downloaded from it.
* An AI Vision model is used to transcribe each page of the pitch deck into markdown.
* An Information Extractor is used to generate a report from the transcribed markdown and update required information back into pitch deck database.
* The transcribed markdown is also uploaded to a vector store to build an AI chatbot which can be used to ask questions on the pitch deck.

Check out the sample Airtable here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### How To Use
* This template depends on the availability of the Airtable - make a duplicate of the airtable ({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and its columns before running the workflow.
* When a new pitchdeck is received, enter the company name into the **Name** column and upload the pdf into the **File** column. Leave all other columns blank.
* If you have the Airtable trigger active, the execution should start immediately once the file is uploaded. Otherwise, click the manual test trigger to start the workflow.
* When manually triggered, all "new" pitch decks will be handled by the workflow as separate executions.

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

Before answering any questions, ensure the user has specified a startup in which they want to enquire about and that the startup pitchdeck exists in the database. If the pitchdeck is not known to us, let the user know.

parameters.content:
## 6. Build a Vector Store Collection for the Pitch Deck
[Read more about Qdrant Vector Store]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

When it comes to pitch deck, it may not be enough to capture static attributes in table. Wouldn't it be cool if we could also ask questions about the pitch deck itself? Well, thanks for n8n's first class support for vector stores, you can and quite easily too!

In this demonstration, we'll use the Qdrant Vector Store which you can either sign-up for a free instance at {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or self-host via docker. Next, it's a simple case of just funneling our transcribed pages into the vector store using the Qdrant Vector Store node.

### Change Me!
You'll need to update the Qdrant URL in the "Delete Existing Vectors" node.

## 3. Split PDF Pages into Seperate Images

Currently, the vision model we'll be using can't accept raw PDFs so we'll have to convert our PDF to a image in order to use it. To achieve this, we'll use the free [Stirling PDF webservice]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} for convenience but if we need data privacy (recommended!), we could self-host our own [Stirling PDF instance]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} instead. Alternatively, feel free to swap this service out for one of your own as long as it can convert PDFs into images!

We will ask the PDF service to return each page of our statement as separate images, which it does so as a zip file. Next steps is to just unzip the file and convert the output as a list of images.

## 4. Convert PDF Pages to Markdown Using Vision Model
[Learn more about using the Basic LLM node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Pitch decks are fundamentally extravagant sales documents and as such, are incredibly varied in how they are styled, structured and presented. Traditional OCR has often struggled with parsing these kinds of documents as layers and graphical elements and when extracting their contents typically yields poor results; either garbled or missing texts.

Multimodal LLMs are a solution as they use AI vision to "read" the pitch deck and can reason about it's layout and intent. Images can be understood and described with context and charts and graphs can also be interpreted. In this demonstration, we'll ask our LLM to transcribe each page in the pitch deck into markdown ensuring it also describes any images or charts it sees.

### Privacy Warning!
This example uses a public third party service. If your data is senstive, please swap this out for the self-hosted version!

## Try It Out!

### This n8n template imports Pitch Decks and generates a report into Airtable as well as creates an AI Chatbot to ask questions about each Pitch Deck.

* Airtable is used as the pitch deck database and PDF decks are downloaded from it.
* An AI Vision model is used to transcribe each page of the pitch deck into markdown.
* An Information Extractor is used to generate a report from the transcribed markdown and update required information back into pitch deck database.
* The transcribed markdown is also uploaded to a vector store to build an AI chatbot which can be used to ask questions on the pitch deck.

Check out the sample Airtable here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### How To Use
* This template depends on the availability of the Airtable - make a duplicate of the airtable ({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and its columns before running the workflow.
* When a new pitchdeck is received, enter the company name into the **Name** column and upload the pdf into the **File** column. Leave all other columns blank.
* If you have the Airtable trigger active, the execution should start immediately once the file is uploaded. Otherwise, click the manual test trigger to start the workflow.
* When manually triggered, all "new" pitch decks will be handled by the workflow as separate executions.

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### Change Me!
Remember to update Airtable nodes to point  to your own.

If two or more tables are adjacent horizontally, stack the tables vertically instead. There should be a newline after every markdown table.
* For any charts, describe the chart type, purpose and result and capture all relevant titles, labels, legends and generaet a table for the datapoints if possible.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
airtable Token  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Verify the settings of the trigger node "Execute Workflow Trigger".
Verify the settings of the trigger node "Airtable Trigger For Pending Rows".
Open the "Sort Pages" node (n8n-nodes-base.sort) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split PDF into Images" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Extract Zip File" node (n8n-nodes-base.compression) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Resize Images For AI" node (n8n-nodes-base.editImage) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Combine All Pages" node (n8n-nodes-base.aggregate) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Default Data Loader" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Recursive Character Text Splitter" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model2" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings OpenAI" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Delete Existing Vectors" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Continue With Pages Only" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Update Pitchdecks Table" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
